content.problemStatement:
Write a program to calculate the sum of the digits present in an alphanumeric string.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer, T, denoting the number of test cases.
Each of the next T lines contains a string consisting of lowercase/uppercase English letters, along with digits 0 to 9. (There shall be no space in the string)


Output Format

For each test case, print the sum of digits in each string.
constraints: Strings contain only printable ASCII characters; no spaces.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty alphanumeric string (letters, digits and other printable ASCII characters) without spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer — the sum of all decimal digits present in the corresponding string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Sum of digits 1 through 9 plus 0 equals 45.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the given string character by character. Whenever a character lies between '0' and '9' we convert it to its numeric value by subtracting the character '0' and add it to a running sum. After processing the whole string we output the sum. This linear scan is optimal because every character must be examined at least once.
solution.timeComplexity: O(L) per test case, where L is the length of the string. Overall O(total length of all strings).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input string.

code.Java: import java.io.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String s=br.readLine();int sum=0;for(int i=0;i<s.length();i++){char c=s.charAt(i);if(c>='0'&&c<='9'){sum+=c-'0';}}sb.append(sum);if(t<T-1) sb.append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
